name: Allen Eager
abstract: Allen Eager (January 10, 1927 – April 13, 2003) was an American jazz tenor and alto saxophonist who also competed in auto racing and took part in LSD experiments.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: My first record date, Kay [sic] Winding, or Kai as he was known later, Shelly Manne, Ed Safranski, and Allen... And Allen Eager.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was my first big record date with Allen Eager... So he hired me to do this record date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I will never forget the time that I was out in California, and he had his own group at a club there, I think the club was called The Last Word, that was where I first saw Allen Eager -- Allen Eager was playing out there at the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: It could be an all-star group with Gerry Mulligan, Kai Winding, and Allen Eager, or Brew Moore, actually.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Allen Eager came in. He didn't stay. And both of them were trying to play like Ben Webster at that time.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I get back to New York with him... and I ran into Alan Allen Eager, a tenor player, a white boy
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
